title: Apply is not transactional
description: Terraform executes resource changes incrementally; a mid-apply failure leaves partially-applied infrastructure and updated state, with no automatic rollback.
body: Use when an apply fails halfway. Unlike a database transaction, Terraform applies the graph node by node and commits each success to state; a failure midway leaves some resources created and others not, and there is no automatic rollback. Do not assume a failed apply changed nothing. Actions: read which resources the error reports as created, run plan to see remaining work, and re-apply or manually reconcile; use create_before_destroy to avoid downtime windows. Validation signals include a follow-up plan that converges and matches intent. Risk boundary: a partial apply on production can leave it in a broken intermediate state — treat a failed apply as an incident requiring deliberate recovery, not a retry-until-green loop.
